prob_desc_input_spec: The input contains a single line containing an integer p (2 ≤ p < 2000). It is guaranteed that p is a prime.
prob_desc_notes: NoteThe only primitive root  is 2.The primitive roots  are 2 and 3.
prob_desc_output_spec: Output on a single line the number of primitive roots .
prob_desc_description: The cows have just learned what a primitive root is! Given a prime p, a primitive root  is an integer x (1 ≤ x < p) such that none of integers x - 1, x2 - 1, ..., xp - 2 - 1 are divisible by p, but xp - 1 - 1 is. Unfortunately, computing primitive roots can be time consuming, so the cows need your help. Given a prime p, help the cows find the number of primitive roots .
